Title: Apparel Mockup On A Real Person (T-Shirt / Hoodie)
Teaser: Drops your design onto a tee or hoodie worn by a believable model in a styled setting, with the fabric folding, wrinkling and catching light exactly as cotton really does. The 'someone is actually wearing this' shot that sells merch.
PromptHint: Attach or describe the [DESIGN/ARTWORK], name the [GARMENT] (e.g. heavyweight tee, pullover hoodie), and optionally set the [GARMENT COLOR] and [SETTING].
Prompt: ROLE: You are an apparel mockup photographer who shoots merch for clothing brands. TASK: Print the supplied [DESIGN/ARTWORK] onto a [GARMENT] worn by a real, relaxed model so it looks like a genuine product photo. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide a body pose and crop that shows the full design flat-on, the natural way the fabric drapes across the chest, and where folds and the seam lines fall. THEN GENERATE: dress a plausible model in a [GARMENT COLOR] [GARMENT] with the artwork printed on the front, scaled and positioned like real screen-printing — the design must follow every wrinkle, fold and curve of the fabric and pick up the same soft light as the cloth around it; place them in [SETTING] with a softly out-of-focus background; light with natural directional light, gentle contact shadows and authentic cotton/fleece texture (visible weave, ribbing on cuffs and collar). CONSTRAINTS: keep the design's colors, proportions and any text exactly as supplied — no warping, mirroring or invented elements; the print sits ON the fabric (matte, follows folds), never a flat sticker floating above it; realistic five-finger hands and natural skin if visible; one garment, no extra logos or text. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished worn-apparel image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw --v 6'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the garment color, pose or setting.

Title: Folded & Flat-Lay Apparel Mockup (Studio Tee Stack)
Teaser: Shows your design on a crisply folded garment or a flat-laid tee shot straight from above on a clean surface — the tidy catalog look that makes a print-on-demand listing feel like a real brand.
PromptHint: Attach or describe the [DESIGN/ARTWORK], name the [GARMENT], and optionally set [GARMENT COLOR] and [SURFACE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a still-life apparel stylist shooting clean catalog flat-lays. TASK: Present the supplied [DESIGN/ARTWORK] on a [GARMENT] either neatly folded or laid flat and photographed top-down for a product listing. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide folded-stack vs flat-laid, where the design lands so it stays fully visible, and how soft shadow gives the cloth volume. THEN GENERATE: render a [GARMENT COLOR] [GARMENT] with the artwork printed on the front, styled either as a crisp fold (with the design centered on the visible panel) or flat-laid wrinkle-light and symmetrical, on a flat [SURFACE]; shoot perfectly overhead or at a gentle catalog angle; light evenly and softly so fabric texture (weave, ribbed collar, stitched hems) reads clearly with gentle, consistent shadows and true color. CONSTRAINTS: keep the design's colors, scale and text exactly as supplied — no distortion or added elements; the print follows the cloth's subtle folds and sheen, never a floating flat sticker; clean even light, no harsh hotspots; one garment, no extra logos or text. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished flat-lay/folded image only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to switch between folded and flat, or change the surface.

Title: Phone / Laptop Screen Mockup (App & Website Showcase)
Teaser: Places your screenshot inside a real device — a phone in hand or a laptop on a desk — with the screen glowing accurately behind glass, plus reflections, shadows and a believable workspace around it. The hero shot for app stores, landing pages and decks.
PromptHint: Attach or describe the [SCREEN CONTENT], name the [DEVICE] (e.g. modern smartphone, thin laptop), and optionally set the [SETTING] and [ANGLE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a product photographer who specializes in clean device mockups for digital products. TASK: Display the supplied [SCREEN CONTENT] inside a [DEVICE] so it reads as a real screenshot on real hardware. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the device angle, whether it's handheld or resting, and how screen glow, glass reflection and ambient light interact. THEN GENERATE: render a current-generation [DEVICE] with the supplied UI filling its screen pixel-crisp and edge-to-edge inside the bezel, viewed at [ANGLE], placed within [SETTING]; let the screen emit a faint glow onto nearby surfaces, add subtle realistic glass reflections that never obscure the UI, a soft contact shadow under the device, and a softly blurred background; keep materials photoreal (matte metal, glass, accurate bezel proportions). CONSTRAINTS: keep the supplied screen content sharp, correctly cropped and undistorted — no warped UI, invented buttons, or gibberish text; accurate device proportions and a screen that conforms to the device's perspective; realistic five-finger hand if held; no added watermarks or extra logos. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished device mockup only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 3:2 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the device angle or the setting.

Title: Box / Carton Packaging Mockup (Folding Cartons & Mailers)
Teaser: Wraps your label artwork around a real folding carton or shipping box, with crisp printed faces, true edge folds, a soft top-light and a grounded shadow — the packshot that makes a retail or DTC product feel shelf-ready.
PromptHint: Attach or describe the [LABEL/ARTWORK], name the [BOX TYPE] (e.g. tuck-end carton, mailer box, kraft shipper), and optionally set [MATERIAL FINISH] and [BACKDROP].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a packaging photographer shooting retail-ready carton mockups. TASK: Apply the supplied [LABEL/ARTWORK] to a [BOX TYPE] so it looks like a printed, manufactured package. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide a three-quarter angle that shows two or three faces, how the artwork wraps and aligns across folds, and where the printed panel breaks at each edge. THEN GENERATE: render the [BOX TYPE] at a clean three-quarter view with the artwork printed accurately on each visible face, aligned to the box geometry and breaking crisply at the fold lines; give it a believable [MATERIAL FINISH] (e.g. matte coated, soft-touch, kraft uncoated); light from the upper-front with one large soft source so each face reads at a slightly different brightness, add a grounded contact shadow and a clean [BACKDROP]; keep cardboard edges, slight thickness and printed color photoreal. CONSTRAINTS: keep the artwork's colors, layout and text exactly as supplied — wrapped to the box, not floating; correct perspective on every face, accurate fold alignment, no melted edges or duplicated panels; one box, no extra props or added text. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished carton mockup only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the finish, angle or backdrop.

Title: Bottle / Jar Label Mockup (Beverage, Cosmetic & Supplement)
Teaser: Curves your flat label artwork around a real bottle or jar — glass or plastic, with accurate liquid level, condensation or a soft sheen, and the label following the cylinder's curve exactly. The hero packshot for drinks, skincare and supplements.
PromptHint: Attach or describe the [LABEL ARTWORK], name the [CONTAINER] (e.g. amber dropper bottle, frosted glass jar, slim soda can), and optionally set [CONTENTS] and [BACKDROP].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a beverage and cosmetics packshot photographer. TASK: Wrap the supplied [LABEL ARTWORK] around a [CONTAINER] so it reads as a finished, fillable product. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide how the label curves around the cylinder (center crisp, edges compressing into the curve), the cap/closure style, the contents level, and the lighting that suits glass vs plastic. THEN GENERATE: render the [CONTAINER] holding [CONTENTS] with the label applied so it bends realistically around the curved surface — sharp and readable at the front, foreshortening toward the sides; add accurate material cues (glass refraction, condensation, soft plastic sheen, a matte or glossy label as appropriate); light with a soft key and a clean edge highlight that defines the silhouette, a subtle reflection and contact shadow on a [BACKDROP]; keep proportions, closure and fill level believable. CONSTRAINTS: keep the label's colors, layout and text exactly as supplied — conformed to the curve, never a flat rectangle pasted on top; physically correct refraction and liquid level, no floating label or distorted text; one container, no added logos or text beyond the label. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished bottle/jar mockup only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the container, contents or backdrop.

Title: Pouch & Flexible Packaging Mockup (Stand-Up Bag / Sachet)
Teaser: Puts your artwork on a stand-up coffee pouch, snack bag or sachet — with the soft creases, gusset, matte-or-foil finish and tiny surface wrinkles that make flexible packaging look genuinely filled and ready to sell.
PromptHint: Attach or describe the [LABEL ARTWORK], name the [POUCH TYPE] (e.g. stand-up coffee pouch, foil sachet, resealable snack bag), and optionally set [FINISH] and [BACKDROP].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a packaging photographer who shoots flexible pouches for food and consumer brands. TASK: Apply the supplied [LABEL ARTWORK] to a [POUCH TYPE] so it looks like a real filled pouch on set. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the fill plumpness, where the side gusset and top seal sit, and how soft creases and the chosen finish catch light. THEN GENERATE: render a plausibly filled [POUCH TYPE] standing upright, with the artwork printed across the front face following the pouch's gentle bulge, soft creases and seal seams; give it a believable [FINISH] (e.g. matte, glossy, brushed-foil) with finish-appropriate highlights; show the gusset, crimped top seal and any zipper realistically; light with a soft key and grounded shadow on a clean [BACKDROP], keeping print color and texture photoreal. CONSTRAINTS: keep the artwork's colors, layout and text exactly as supplied — conformed to the pouch's curves and creases, not a flat sticker; realistic seams, gusset and material sheen; legible front panel, no warped or duplicated text; one pouch, no extra props or added logos. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished pouch mockup only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the pouch type, finish or backdrop.

Title: Outdoor Billboard Mockup (Highway & Urban Placement)
Teaser: Maps your ad creative onto a real billboard in a believable street or highway scene — correct perspective, frame, daylight or city-night glow, and the grit of the real world around it. The 'see it live in the wild' mockup for campaign pitches.
PromptHint: Attach or describe the [AD CREATIVE], pick the [LOCATION] (e.g. busy highway, downtown intersection, subway corridor), and optionally set [TIME OF DAY] and [BILLBOARD TYPE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are an out-of-home (OOH) advertising visualizer who mocks up billboards for client pitches. TASK: Place the supplied [AD CREATIVE] onto a [BILLBOARD TYPE] within a realistic [LOCATION] scene. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the viewing angle and distance, how the creative skews into the billboard's perspective, and how ambient light and the surrounding environment frame it. THEN GENERATE: build a believable [LOCATION] at [TIME OF DAY] with a [BILLBOARD TYPE] holding the supplied creative — the artwork conformed exactly to the board's perspective and proportions, sitting flush inside the frame; match the creative's brightness and color temperature to the scene's lighting (sunlit, overcast, or backlit night glow), add the board's structure, subtle weathering and real-world surroundings (road, buildings, sky, faint traffic), with natural depth and atmosphere. CONSTRAINTS: keep the creative's colors, layout and text exactly as supplied — skewed to perspective, never flat or floating; photoreal scale and placement (the board is part of the scene, not pasted on); realistic environment, no gibberish signage or warped text on the creative; one primary billboard. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished billboard mockup only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 16:9 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the location, time of day or board type.

Title: Retail Shelf & In-Store Mockup (On-Shelf Context)
Teaser: Stands your packaged product on a real store shelf surrounded by softly blurred neighbors — the context shot that proves it pops at point-of-sale and shows buyers exactly how it'll look in the wild.
PromptHint: Attach or describe the [PRODUCT PACKAGE], pick the [STORE TYPE] (e.g. grocery aisle, beauty boutique, cafe counter), and optionally set [SHELF STYLE] and [LIGHTING MOOD].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a retail and shopper-marketing photographer shooting on-shelf product context. TASK: Place the supplied [PRODUCT PACKAGE] on a realistic [STORE TYPE] shelf so it looks photographed in-aisle. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the shelf eye-line, how the hero product sits front-and-facing, and how to blur neighbors so the hero stays sharp. THEN GENERATE: render the [PRODUCT PACKAGE] standing label-forward on a [SHELF STYLE] shelf within a [STORE TYPE], with a few softly out-of-focus neighboring products and shelf details receding into shallow depth; light with believable [LIGHTING MOOD] retail lighting (even overheads or warm boutique glow), accurate shadows, reflections on the shelf edge, and true package color; keep the hero crisp, correctly proportioned and clearly the focal point. CONSTRAINTS: keep the package's design, label and proportions exactly as supplied — no relabeling or warping; neighbors must be generic and blurred (no real competitor brands or legible foreign text); photoreal shelf, lighting and depth; one hero product, no added overlay text. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished on-shelf mockup only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 3:2 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the store type, shelf style or lighting.

Title: Stationery & Brand Identity Mockup Set (Cards, Letterhead, Tote)
Teaser: Lays your logo and brand across a curated set of touchpoints — business card, letterhead, envelope, tote or mug — in one cohesive flat-lay that makes a fresh identity feel like a fully built brand.
PromptHint: Attach or describe the [LOGO/BRAND ARTWORK], list the [ITEMS] to include (e.g. business card, letterhead, tote, mug), and optionally set [PALETTE] and [SURFACE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a brand-identity art director styling a cohesive stationery mockup set. TASK: Apply the supplied [LOGO/BRAND ARTWORK] across a curated group of [ITEMS] arranged as one polished presentation. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the hero item, the arrangement (tidy flat-lay or staged scene), and how the palette ties the pieces together with breathing room. THEN GENERATE: compose the [ITEMS] together on a [SURFACE], each carrying the brand artwork applied correctly to its material — ink on paper for cards and letterhead, print on fabric for a tote, ceramic print on a mug — using the [PALETTE] consistently; shoot top-down or at a gentle angle, light softly and evenly with realistic paper texture, fabric weave, ceramic sheen, subtle shadows and tasteful negative space; keep every logo crisp, correctly scaled and color-true on each surface. CONSTRAINTS: keep the logo and brand artwork exactly as supplied — printed onto each material's texture (not floating stickers), no distortion or invented elements; realistic materials and lighting; cohesive, uncluttered composition; only the listed items, no extra random props or added text. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished stationery set mockup only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 1:1 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to swap an item, change the palette or the surface.

Title: Book / Magazine Cover Mockup (Hardcover, Paperback & Spread)
Teaser: Turns your flat cover art into a real book or magazine — standing hardcover with thickness and pages, a held paperback, or an open spread — with paper sheen, spine shadow and grounded light. The hero shot for launches and ads.
PromptHint: Attach or describe the [COVER ARTWORK], pick the [FORMAT] (e.g. standing hardcover, held paperback, open magazine spread), and optionally set [PAPER FINISH] and [BACKDROP].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a publishing photographer who shoots book and magazine mockups for launches. TASK: Render the supplied [COVER ARTWORK] as a real [FORMAT] so it looks like a printed, physical title. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the angle, whether the spine and page edges show, and how the paper finish catches light. THEN GENERATE: build the [FORMAT] with the cover art applied exactly to its front (and wrapped onto the spine if visible), giving it believable thickness, visible page edges, a slight cover curve, and a [PAPER FINISH] (e.g. matte, gloss, soft-touch) with finish-appropriate sheen; light with a soft key and a clean edge highlight, add a grounded contact shadow and a subtle reflection on a [BACKDROP]; for a spread, show two facing pages with a natural center gutter and gentle page curl. CONSTRAINTS: keep the cover's colors, typography and layout exactly as supplied — conformed to the cover's slight curve and perspective, never flat-pasted; readable title, no warped or duplicated text; realistic paper, spine and page edges; one title, no extra props or added logos. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished book/magazine mockup only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the format, paper finish or backdrop.

Title: Cap, Mug & Tote Merch Mockup (Wrapped-Surface Goods)
Teaser: Wraps your logo or design around a curved product — an embroidered cap, a printed mug, a canvas tote — so it follows the real curve, stitch or weave and sits in a lifestyle scene. The everyday merch packshot that looks like it shipped.
PromptHint: Attach or describe the [DESIGN/LOGO], name the [PRODUCT] (e.g. embroidered cap, ceramic mug, canvas tote), and optionally set [PRODUCT COLOR] and [SETTING].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a merchandise photographer who shoots branded everyday goods. TASK: Apply the supplied [DESIGN/LOGO] to a [PRODUCT] so it reads as a real, manufactured merch item. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide how the design conforms to this product's surface — embroidery raised on a cap's curved front, glaze-print wrapping a mug's cylinder, screen-print sitting flat on a tote's weave — plus the angle and setting. THEN GENERATE: render the [PRODUCT COLOR] [PRODUCT] with the design applied in the correct technique for its material: embroidery with visible thread texture on a cap, a print that curves around a mug and foreshortens toward its sides, or ink that sinks into canvas weave on a tote; place it in [SETTING] with a softly blurred background; light with a soft key, a clean edge highlight, accurate material texture (fabric weave, ceramic sheen, stitch relief) and a grounded shadow. CONSTRAINTS: keep the design's colors, scale and text exactly as supplied — conformed to the product's curve, stitch or weave, never a flat floating sticker; realistic material and finish; legible, undistorted artwork; one product, no extra logos or added text. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished merch mockup only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the product, color or setting.

Title: Unboxing Scene Mockup (Open Box, Tissue & Insert Reveal)
Teaser: Stages the moment the lid comes off — your product nestled in tissue inside a branded box, with a card, packing detail and warm overhead light. The premium 'first impression' shot for DTC brands and gifting.
PromptHint: Attach or describe the [PRODUCT] and any [BOX/INSERT ARTWORK], pick the [UNBOXING STYLE] (e.g. luxury tissue reveal, eco kraft fill, gift set), and optionally set [SURFACE] and [MOOD].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a packaging-experience photographer who shoots DTC unboxing scenes. TASK: Stage the supplied [PRODUCT] inside an open branded box mid-reveal in a [UNBOXING STYLE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the overhead-or-three-quarter angle, how the lid, tissue and insert frame the product, and the warm light that makes it feel like a real first moment. THEN GENERATE: render an open box with the lid lifted or set aside, the [PRODUCT] nestled in protective fill appropriate to the [UNBOXING STYLE] (crisp tissue and a thank-you card for luxury, kraft shred for eco, a tray of items for a gift set); apply any supplied [BOX/INSERT ARTWORK] accurately to the box interior, lid or card following each surface; place it on a [SURFACE] with a [MOOD]; light from above with a soft, warm key, gentle shadows and tactile material texture (paper, tissue, cardboard, the product's own finish). CONSTRAINTS: keep the product and any supplied artwork exactly as given — artwork printed onto the box/card surfaces, not floating; photoreal materials, fill and shadows; a believable, uncluttered reveal; one hero product, no fake brand text beyond what's supplied. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished unboxing scene only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 4:5 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the unboxing style, surface or mood.

Title: Storefront Signage & Window Mockup (Brick-And-Mortar Brand)
Teaser: Mounts your logo on a real storefront — a sign band, a window decal or an awning — in a believable street scene with daylight, glass reflections and passersby. The mockup that shows a brand living on an actual street.
PromptHint: Attach or describe the [LOGO/SIGN ARTWORK], pick the [STOREFRONT TYPE] (e.g. cafe, boutique, salon), and optionally set [SIGN PLACEMENT] (sign band, window decal, awning, blade sign) and [STREET MOOD].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a signage and environmental-branding visualizer who mocks up storefronts for clients. TASK: Apply the supplied [LOGO/SIGN ARTWORK] to a [STOREFRONT TYPE] at the chosen [SIGN PLACEMENT] within a real street scene. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the street-level viewing angle, how the logo sits on the chosen surface (raised channel letters, a flat window decal, printed awning, or a projecting blade sign), and how daylight and glass reflections behave. THEN GENERATE: build a believable [STOREFRONT TYPE] facade in a [STREET MOOD] scene, with the supplied artwork mounted at [SIGN PLACEMENT] — conformed to that surface's perspective and material (dimensional lettering with subtle depth and shadow, a semi-transparent window decal over glass, or print on fabric/awning); add real-world context (sidewalk, glass with soft reflections, greenery, faint passersby) and natural directional light with grounded shadows; keep the logo crisp, correctly scaled and color-true. CONSTRAINTS: keep the logo/sign artwork exactly as supplied — mounted to the storefront in correct perspective, never flat-pasted or floating; photoreal facade, glass, materials and light; no gibberish signage elsewhere, no warped logo text; one primary sign placement. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished storefront mockup only, then on a new line a Midjourney-style param line I can paste (e.g. '--ar 3:2 --style raw'), then one sentence I can paste back to change the storefront type, sign placement or street mood.
